MINI MINUTES: Vivian Lerche, Esq. appearing specially for Defendant. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to Nov-14-2017 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. JUDGE A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; Clerk KTom. Proceedings not reported. (610)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel Sterrett, Esq. (310-613-8397) for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Lorraine Walsh, Esq. (925-932-7014) for Defendant through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues driving the wrong way is not sufficient. Plaintiff argues they have met the standard. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRENDAN LANDECK's MOTION TO STRIKE REQUEST FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES FROM COMPLAINT. Defendant Landeck's motion to strike the complaint's request for punitive damages is DENIED. Plaintiff alleges defendant injured her while driving intoxicated the wrong way on a one-way street. Punitive damages have been held available under like circumstances. (See, e.g., Peterson v. Sup. Ct. (1982) 31 Cal.3d 147, 150; Taylor v. Sup. Ct. (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 896; Dawes v. Sup. Ct. (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 82, 87-88.) (as fully repored in the reporter's notes.)Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/RBU)
